title: asperula hirsuta subsp. rupestris
intro: Asperula hirsuta subsp. rupestris, also known as Alpine Woodruff, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its essential oils, which contain compounds like asperuloside and iridoid glycosides, showing antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Alpine Woodruff include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various gastrointestinal issues and wounds. Alpine Woodruff is often prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be used in the form of a salve or cream for topical applications.
